html:
Carl Alexander Clerck, likely born in Stockholm around 1709 and died there on July 22, 1765, was a Swedish entomologist.

== Biography == Carl Alexander Clerck was the son of bookkeeper Alexander Clerck and Elisabeth Gram; his father belonged to a non-noble branch of the Clerck family (number 1382). Clerck served as a commissioner at Stockholm's city mediation commission and became a member of the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences in Uppsala before 1757, and a member of the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences in 1764. He had a strong interest in the natural history of invertebrates, particularly spiders.

== See also == Swedish Spiders.

== Bibliography ==

At his academy for illustrating and painting natural history no. 10 Great Malborough street London. 1793. Libris 2900709

How certain are we about this?

Information about people from the Age of Liberty is well-documented through parliamentary records, letters, and archives. However, interpretations of their roles are often influenced by later political biases.
tldr: Carl Alexander Clerck was a Swedish researcher who studied insects and spiders during the 18th century.
lattlast:
Carl Alexander Clerck was born in Stockholm around 1709 and died there in 1765. He worked in the field of natural history, which involves studying the functions and appearances of animals and plants.

He was particularly interested in small animals and spent a great deal of time studying spiders. During his lifetime, he was also a member of important scientific societies in both Uppsala and Stockholm.

Throughout his career as a researcher, he published several books about insects and spiders. His writings often contained detailed descriptions and illustrations of the animals he studied.
